intro: Pteryxia thapsoides, also known as California carrot or nuttall's desert parsley, is a perennial herb native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders and other conditions. The bioactive constituents of Pteryxia thapsoides include furanocoumarins, such as bergapten and xanthotoxin, as well as furanocoumarin glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
